item.title: DevLog #2 - Java 8 Support, Bug Fixes & Community
item.description: After a focused sprint of fixes, refactoring, and documentation work, Diogenes 1.0.17 is live. This update is one of the most important since the project launched, not because of new features, but because of how much more stable, compatible, and accessible the ecosystem became.
item.content:encoded:
After a focused sprint of fixes, refactoring, and documentation work, Diogenes 1.0.17 is live. This update is one of the most important since the project launched, not because of new features, but because of how much more stable, compatible, and accessible the ecosystem became.

Here's everything that changed.

☕ Java 8 Support​

The biggest change in this release: the SDK now supports Java 8 and above.

Previously, the SDK required Java 21, which excluded a significant portion of the Minecraft server ecosystem, especially older 1.8.x servers that still run on legacy JDKs.

Starting from 1.0.17, the SDK is fully compatible with:

Java VersionSupported
Java 8✅
Java 11✅
Java 17✅
Java 21✅

This change required a complete rework of how the fat JAR is assembled. More on that below:

🐛 Bug Fixes​

This release resolves three critical issues that were introduced during the Java 8 migration. They appeared sequentially — each fix revealed the next — but all share the same root cause: how Kotlin libraries were being bundled and relocated inside the SDK JAR.

Fix #1 — ExceptionInInitializerError on authentication (1.0.15 → 1.0.16)

What happened: The kotlin-reflect library was being relocated inside the fat JAR (e.g. kotlin.* → dev.efelleto.diogenes.libs.kotlin.*). This broke an internal mechanism in kotlin-reflect that loads built-in type metadata from .kotlin_builtins resource files using hardcoded classpath paths.

Error:

Caused by: java.lang.IllegalStateException: Resource not found in classpath:
dev/efelleto/diogenes/libs/kotlin/...kotlin_builtins

Fix: kotlin-reflect is now fully excluded from the fat JAR. Since it modifies internal JVM behavior and relies on hardcoded resource paths, it cannot be safely relocated.

Fix #2 — ClassNotFoundException: kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope (1.0.16 → 1.0.17)

What happened: After excluding kotlin-reflect, kotlinx-coroutines was also missing from the JAR. The Ktor CIO engine depends heavily on coroutines, and since the target servers (1.8.8 vanilla) don't provide Kotlin at runtime, the class was simply not found.

Error:

java.lang.ClassNotFoundException: kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope

Fix: kotlinx-coroutines-core is now bundled inside the fat JAR without relocation, ensuring it's always available regardless of the server environment.

Fix #3 — Banner log pollution on server startup (1.0.17)

What happened: The Diogenes ASCII banner was being printed inside a runTaskLater (10 tick delay), which caused Spigot's own startup messages to interleave with the banner output in the console.

Before:

__________________________________________________________________________________
    _____^_
   ...
__________________________________________________________________________________
[INFO]: The server will be available in 4 seconds.   ← intruder
[INFO]: The server will be available in 3 seconds.   ← intruder
[INFO]: AUTH: Successfully authenticated.

After:

__________________________________________________________________________________
    _____^_
   ...
__________________________________________________________________________________

[INFO]: The server will be available in 4 seconds.
[INFO]: The server will be available in 3 seconds.
[INFO]: AUTH: Successfully authenticated.

Fix: The banner is now printed synchronously during onEnable(), before any async task is scheduled.

📦 SDK Build Rework​

The entire fat JAR assembly strategy was redesigned. Here's the final configuration that makes everything work cleanly:

LibraryStrategyReason
kotlin-stdlibBundled, not relocatedServer doesn't provide it
kotlin-reflectExcludedBreaks with any relocation
kotlinx-coroutinesBundled, not relocatedRequired by Ktor CIO
kotlinx.atomicfuBundled + relocatedTransitive dep, may conflict
io.ktorBundled + relocatedAvoid classpath conflicts
com.google.gsonBundled + relocatedAvoid classpath conflicts

📚 Documentation Overhaul​

All documentation pages were reviewed and updated to reflect the current state of the project:

  • Getting Started: Java requirement corrected to Java 8+, Gradle snippet fixed to proper Kotlin DSL syntax.
  • Integration: Fully rewritten with a parameter reference table, step-by-step authentication flow, and a complete error handling table.
  • Installation: Fixed inconsistency between config.yml and settings.yml references. Configuration block is now a proper YAML example.
  • Discord Commands: Fixed header hierarchy and standardized formatting.
  • Security Flow: Minor formatting cleanup.

💬 Community​

The Diogenes Discord server is being set up. It will be the central hub for:

  • Getting help with installation and integration
  • Reporting bugs and suggesting features
  • Following development updates in real time
  • Connecting with other developers using the ecosystem

An invite link will be shared here and in the README as soon as the server is ready. Stay tuned.

🗺️ What's Next​

  • Sinope Layer: JAR obfuscation CLI tool. The groundwork is laid, active development starts next.
  • Discord invite: Community server going live soon.

Thanks for following the project. If Diogenes has been useful to you, consider leaving a ⭐ on GitHub, it helps a lot.

— Eduardo Felleto

item.title: DevLog #3 - Server config simplification & SDK Fix
item.description: Two small but meaningful releases back to back. 1.0.18 simplifies the server configuration system, and 1.0.19 fixes a critical SDK bug affecting servers running multiple licensed plugins simultaneously.
item.content:encoded:
Two small but meaningful releases back to back. 1.0.18 simplifies the server configuration system, and 1.0.19 fixes a critical SDK bug affecting servers running multiple licensed plugins simultaneously.

1.0.18 - Server Config Simplification​

🗂️ The Old Structure​

Previously, the server generated a nested folder structure on first run:

diogenes-config/
├── server/
│   └── config.yml
└── discord/
    └── messages.yml

This made sense in theory, but in practice it was unnecessary overhead. The messages.yml file contained Discord embed messages that are better handled at the Sinope layer, and the extra folders added friction for anyone setting up the server quickly.

✅ The New Structure​

Starting from 1.0.18, the server generates a single file at the root, right next to the JAR:

DiogenesServer.jar
config.yml          ← generated here on first run

The config file itself was also cleaned up. Two unused fields (admin-role-id and log-channel-id) were removed:

server:
  port: 8080
  mongo-uri: "mongodb://localhost:27017"

discord:
  bot-token: "YOUR_BOT_TOKEN_HERE"

🖥️ First Boot Messages​

The first-run console output now follows the same visual standard as the rest of the server logs:

Before:

[!] Config folder created on: /home/user/diogenes-config
[!] Please fill the information in ./diogenes-config/server/config.yml and restart.

After:

[ INFO ] CONFIG: File created at: /home/user/config.yml
[ INFO ] CONFIG: Please fill config.yml and restart the server.

⚠️ Migration Note​

If you are upgrading from a previous version, your old diogenes-config/server/config.yml will not be read automatically. Before restarting with 1.0.18:

  1. Copy your mongo-uri and bot-token values from the old file.
  2. Delete the diogenes-config/ folder.
  3. Run the new JAR — it will generate a fresh config.yml at the root.
  4. Paste your credentials and restart.

1.0.19 — SDK Singleton Fix​

🐛 The Problem​

When two or more plugins using the Diogenes SDK were loaded on the same server, they shared the same SDK instance — because DiogenesSDK was implemented as a Kotlin object (singleton).

This caused the second plugin to overwrite the pluginFolder of the first, making both plugins read from the same license.yml file. In practice, this meant the second plugin could authenticate using the first plugin's key without having its own license configured.

[ INFO ] AUTH: Successfully authenticated. PRODUCT: plugin1     ← correct
[ INFO ] AUTH: Successfully authenticated. PRODUCT: plugin2     ← wrong, used plugin1's key

✅ The Fix​

DiogenesSDK was refactored from an object to a class. Each init() call now creates an isolated instance with its own productId, baseUrl, and pluginFolder.

plugins/Plugin1/license.yml     ← read by plugin1's instance
plugins/Plugin2/license.yml     ← read by plugin2's instance

The public API is unchanged — your onEnable() code stays exactly the same:

DiogenesSDK.init(this, "your-product-id", "http://your-server:8080", () -> { ... });

🎨 Banner Improvement​

The ASCII banner now prints together with the authentication result, instead of appearing separately at the top of the log:

__________________________________________________________________________________

    _____^_
   ...
   / \_/   0  \        Diogenes 1.0.19

[ INFO ] AUTH: Successfully authenticated. PRODUCT: plugin
__________________________________________________________________________________

If multiple plugins are loaded, only the first one prints the banner. Subsequent plugins print only their result line — keeping the console clean.

— Eduardo Felleto

item.title: Welcome to the Diogenes DevLog
item.description: Welcome to the official Diogenes DevLog. This is where we will document the evolution of our licensing ecosystem.
item.content:encoded:
Welcome to the official Diogenes DevLog. This is where we will document the evolution of our licensing ecosystem.

Today marks a major milestone as we've finalized the documentation for the Server and SDK modules. Our goal is to provide a seamless and secure experience for developers who want to protect their intellectual property without the headache of complex integrations.

What's coming next?​

  • Sinope Layer: Advanced obfuscation for JAR files.

Stay tuned for more updates!
